bat the village issued 240 tokens in one year, each with a value of 3 pence, the total value of the tokens is 240 × 3 = 720 pence. the average purchase cost was 18 pence, so the number of average purchases is 720 ÷ 18 = 40. the total number of tokens issued by the village in that year is 240, and each token could be used for one purchase, so 240 purchases could be made with the total number of tokens issued by the village in that year. the historian found that the village used these tokens to pay for goods, with an average purchase costing 18 pence. the total value of the tokens is 240 × 3 = 720 pence, and the average purchase cost was 18 pence, so the number of average purchases is 720 ÷ 18 = 40. the total number of tokens issued by the village in that year is 240, and each token could be used for one purchase, so 240 purchases could be made with the total number of tokens issued by the village in that year. p.p.s. lake
